description: Golden test vectors for Primust canonical JSON serialization. Both TypeScript and Python implementations MUST produce identical output for every valid vector and MUST reject every invalid vector.

vectors.description: Nested object keys sorted recursively at every depth
Three levels of nesting, all sorted recursively
Objects inside arrays have their keys sorted
Null values are preserved as null
Boolean true/false serialized as JSON literals
Strings with characters that require JSON escaping
Complex mixed structure with nested arrays and objects
Unicode characters pass through without escaping (UTF-8 output)
Output must have no spaces, no newlines, no indentation

hash_vectors.description: SHA-256 of canonical form of a flat object

invalid_vectors.description: Non-JSON-native types must raise, not coerce. If the language has a callable/function type, passing one must throw.
invalid_vectors.note: Language-specific: TypeScript should reject functions; Python should reject arbitrary objects with no __json__ protocol.

invalid_vectors.description: Date objects must not be silently coerced to strings. Caller must convert to ISO 8601 string before passing.
invalid_vectors.note: Both languages: new Date() / datetime.now() must raise TypeError or equivalent.

invalid_vectors.description: Raw bytes/Buffer/Uint8Array must not be silently coerced. Caller must base64url-encode first.
invalid_vectors.note: TypeScript: Uint8Array/Buffer must throw. Python: bytes must throw.

invalid_vectors.description: NaN is not valid JSON. Must raise.
invalid_vectors.note: Both languages: NaN must throw, not serialize as null or string.

invalid_vectors.description: Infinity is not valid JSON. Must raise.
